titleStmt.title: Letter from Cis M. Coyle to James Ryan, c. August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Cis M. Coyle

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Cis M. Coyle to James Ryan (1891-1970). The letter was written after Ryan's release from Frongach. The writer is looking to make arrangements to meet Ryan before he returns home to Wexford.James Ryan (1891-1970) studied medicine in UCD. He was appointed chief medical officer in the GPO during the Easter Rising. After the Rising he was imprisoned in Stafford and, later, Frongoch and was released in August 1916. Ryan was later a founder member of Fianna Fáil and a government minister.

correspAction.placeName: Fingall Arms, Dunshaughlin, County Meath, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Tingall Arms Dunshaughlin Co Meath Thursday night My dear Jim I sent you a card
from town to-day
I hope you wont go
home without seeing
me. I will be home
on Monday P.G.
at 5.30. Fr. O'Brien
did not know
when you are going
home but you can
stay here after Monday
can you? I would
be glad to hear
from you in any
case. Excuse pencil
there are some people
here & I have to
scribble a line in
a hurry Any chance
of Liam or Michael
calling home? I
had a letter from
the Councillor this
morning Kindest
regards from all Very sincerely yours Cis M Coyle
